engine_state.analysis_reason: no web interface or Android-specific layout detected

top_candidates.name: lib::libdiag.so
top_candidates.vuln_summary: pre-auth FILE Config-File Buffer Overflow → system
next_steps: In Ghidra search Strings for `diag: In %s, mask sync error, count: %d`. The function referencing it leads to system, printf.
Identify the intermediate hop between input and sink. Look for nvram_get/mib_get/config_get calls immediately before the sink; confirm the key was written with attacker-controlled data.

top_candidates.name: lib::liblbcmnlibs.so
top_candidates.vuln_summary: post-auth FILE Config-File Buffer Overflow → fprintf
next_steps: In Ghidra search Strings for `%s: STA stats callback already installed (func %p, cook`. The function referencing it leads to fprintf, memcpy.
Trace `nvram_get`/`mib_get` calls with keys "vap_seting_lock". Confirm the returned value reaches fprintf, memcpy without sanitisation.
HTTP params `ack_rssi, rssi, ackRssiEnable` are attacker-controlled. Confirm they reach fprintf, memcpy without sanitisation.
Exploitation: no stack canary → overflows not detected at runtime.
Confirm auth requirement: locate the session/cookie check before the vulnerable handler in Ghidra. If absent, the path may be pre-auth; if present, test whether the check is bypassable.

top_candidates.name: lib::libtirpc.so
top_candidates.vuln_summary: post-auth HTTP Stack Buffer Overflow → syslog
next_steps: Load `libtirpc.so` in Ghidra. Search Imports for syslog, sprintf and trace all callers.
Trace `nvram_get`/`mib_get` calls with keys "fwrite". Confirm the returned value reaches syslog, sprintf without sanitisation.
Exploitation: no stack canary → overflows not detected at runtime.
Identify the specific form parameter that reaches the sink. Check the HTML frontend for field names; search those names in Ghidra to confirm they appear in the same function as the sink call.
Confirm auth requirement: locate the session/cookie check before the vulnerable handler in Ghidra. If absent, the path may be pre-auth; if present, test whether the check is bypassable.

top_candidates.name: lib::libgssglue.so
top_candidates.vuln_summary: post-auth FILE Dynamic Library Injection → syslog
next_steps: Load `libgssglue.so` in Ghidra. Search Imports for syslog, sprintf and trace all callers.
Exploitation: no stack canary → overflows not detected at runtime.
Identify the specific form parameter that reaches the sink. Check the HTML frontend for field names; search those names in Ghidra to confirm they appear in the same function as the sink call.
Confirm auth requirement: locate the session/cookie check before the vulnerable handler in Ghidra. If absent, the path may be pre-auth; if present, test whether the check is bypassable.
Verify how this binary is invoked from the web stack. Search CGI scripts and web configs for the binary name, or trace the HTTP dispatcher's handler table in Ghidra.
